title: pellaea occidentalis subsp. simplex
intro: Pellaea occidentalis subsp. simplex is a subspecies of the Pellaea occidentalis fern, a type of plant native to the Americas and known for its edible and medicinal properties. The plant's fronds and rhizomes have been used in herbal medicine for centuries, particularly in traditional Native American practices, where they are valued for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. Pellaea occidentalis subsp. simplex has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, and its medicinal properties have also been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. The plant can be prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and its rhizomes have been used to make a poultice or salve for topical application.
